title: festuca ovina subsp. ingrata
intro: Festuca ovina subsp. ingrata, also known as the bitter sheep's fescue, is a subspecies of grass native to Europe. It has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its essential oils, which have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and soothing respiratory problems, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of Festuca ovina subsp. ingrata, such as infusions or tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
